heading: Create a Stream Processor
body:
To create a stream processor that persists until you drop it:

**Admin-api:**

Atlas Administration API

The Atlas Administration API provides an endpoint for
creating a stream processor.

[Create One Stream Processor](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupstreamprocessor)

**Atlas UI:**

Atlas UI

To create a stream processor in the Atlas UI, go to the
Stream Processing page for your Atlas project and
click Manage in the pane for your stream processing workspace.

You can choose between using the Visual Builder or the JSON (Javascript Object Notation) editor to
configure your stream processor:

Visual Builder

If there are existing stream processors in your stream processing workspace,
click the + Create stream processor button, then
select Visual Builder from the drop-down
options.

The Visual Builder opens with a form where you can
configure your stream processor.

In the Source field, select a connection from
the Connection drop-down list to use as the
source for your stream processor.

This opens a JSON (Javascript Object Notation) text box where you can configure the
`source` stage for your stream processor. To learn
more about `source` stage syntax, see [$source](pipe.$source).

> **Example:** The following `source` stage operates on
> real-time data from the pre-configured
> `sample_stream_solar` connection:
>
> ```json
> {
>   "$source": {
>     "connectionName": "sample_stream_solar"
>   }
> }
> ```

In the Start building your pipeline pane, click
the button for the aggregation stage that you want to add to your
pipeline. This opens a text box where you can configure the
selected aggregation stage in JSON (Javascript Object Notation) format.

If your aggregation stage isn't listed, click
\+ Custom stage to define a
[Aggregation pipeline stages](atlas-sp-aggregation)
in JSON (Javascript Object Notation) format. To learn more about stream processing
aggregation stages and their syntax, see
[Aggregation pipeline stages](atlas-sp-aggregation).

> **Example:** The following [$match](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/match) stage matches all
> documents in the pre-configured `sample_stream_solar`
> stream where the `obs.watts` field is greater than
> `300`:
>
> ```json
> {
>   "$match": {
>     "obs.watts": { "$gt": 300 }
>   }
> }
> ```

To add additional aggregation stages to your pipeline, click
the + Add stage below button below the last
stage in your pipeline, and select the aggregation stage
that you want to add or click Custom stage to
define a different [Aggregation pipeline stages](atlas-sp-aggregation). This opens a text box where you can
configure the new stage in JSON (Javascript Object Notation) format.

In the Sink field, select a destination
connection from the Connection drop-down list.

In the Sink field, select a connection from the
Connection drop-down list to write your
processed data to.

This opens a JSON (Javascript Object Notation) text box where you can configure the
`merge` stage for your stream processor. To learn
more about `merge` stage syntax, see [$merge](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/merge).

> **Example:** The following `sink` stage write processed data to the
> `demoDb.demoColl` collection in a connection named
> `demoConnection` connection:
>
> ```json
> {
>   "$merge": {
>     "into": {
>       "connectionName": "demoConnection",
>       "db": "demoDb",
>       "coll": "demoColl"
>     }
>   }
> }
> ```

The stream processor is created and listed on the
Stream Processors tab of the Stream
Processing page.

JSON Editor

If there are existing stream processors in your stream processing workspace,
click the + Create stream processor button, then
select Visual Builder from the drop-down
options.

The JSON editor opens with a text box where you can
configure your stream processor in JSON (Javascript Object Notation) format.

Specify the JSON (Javascript Object Notation) definition for your stream processor in
the JSON editor text box. This definition must include a
name for your stream processor and an aggregation pipeline
that starts with a [$source](pipe.$source) stage and ends with
the [$merge](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/merge) stage. You can include any number of
additional aggregation stages between the `$source` and
`$merge` stages.

To learn more about stream processing aggregation stages and
their syntax, see [Aggregation pipeline stages](atlas-sp-aggregation).

> **Example:** The following JSON (Javascript Object Notation) definition creates a stream
> processor named `solarDemo` that uses a
> [$tumblingWindow](pipe.$tumblingWindow) stage with a nested
> [$group](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/group) stage to aggregate real-time data from
> the pre-configured `sample_stream_solar` connection
> over 10-second intervals, and writes the processed data
> to a collection in a connection named `mongodb1`.
>
> ```json
> {
>   "name": "solarDemo",
>   "pipeline": [
>     {
>       "$source": {
>         "connectionName": "sample_stream_solar"
>       }
>     },
>     {
>       "$tumblingWindow": {
>         "interval": {
>           "size": 10,
>           "unit": "second"
>         },
>         "pipeline": [
>           {
>             "$group": {
>               "_id": "$group_id",
>               "max_watts": { "$max": "$obs.watts" },
>               "min_watts": { "$min": "$obs.watts" }
>             }
>           }
>         ]
>       }
>     },
>     {
>       "$merge": {
>         "into": {
>           "connectionName": "mongodb1",
>           "db": "solarDb",
>           "coll": "solarColl"
>         }
>       }
>     }
>   ]
> }
> ```

**mongosh:**

`mongosh`

To create a new stream processor with [mongosh](mongodb-shell), use the
[sp.createStreamProcessor()](manual/reference/method/sp.createStreamProcessor) method. It has the following syntax:

```sh
sp.createStreamProcessor(<name>, <pipeline>, <options>)
```

| Argument                   | Type   | Necessity   | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| -------------------------- | ------ | ----------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| name                       | string | Required    | Logical name for the stream processor. This must be unique within the stream processing workspace. This name should contain only alphanumeric characters.                                                                                          |
| pipeline                   | array  | Required    | Stream aggregation pipeline you want to apply to your streaming data.                                                                                                                                                                              |
| options                    | object | Optional    | Object defining various optional settings for your stream processor.                                                                                                                                                                               |
| options.dlq                | object | Conditional | Object assigning a dead letter queue for your stream processing workspace. This field is necessary if you define the options field.                                                                                                                |
| options.dlq.connectionName | string | Conditional | Human-readable label that identifies a connection in your connection registry. This connection must reference an Atlas cluster. This field is necessary if you define the options.dlq field.                                                       |
| options.dlq.db             | string | Conditional | Name of an Atlas database on the cluster specified in options.dlq.connectionName. This field is necessary if you define the options.dlq field.                                                                                                     |
| options.dlq.coll           | string | Conditional | Name of a collection in the database specified in options.dlq.db. This field is necessary if you define the options.dlq field.                                                                                                                     |
| options.tier               | string | Optional    | The tier of the pod to which Atlas Stream Processing assigns the processor. If you do not declare this option, Atlas Stream Processing assigns the processor to a pod of the stream processing workspace's default tier. To learn more, see Tiers. |
